output:
CVE-2020-35170 describes a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Dell EMC Unisphere for PowerMax and PowerMax OS. This vulnerability affects versions prior to 9.1.0.9 and 9.0.2.16 of Unisphere, as well as versions 5978.221.221 and 5978.479.479 of PowerMax OS.

The vulnerability, identified as CWE-79, arises from insufficient input sanitization, allowing an authenticated malicious user to inject JavaScript into the application. This can potentially impact other authenticated users by hijacking their sessions.

The severity of this vulnerability is rated as medium. It is associated with the MITRE ATT&CK technique T1190, which involves exploitation of public-facing applications.

Affected software should be updated to the latest versions beyond those specified to mitigate this risk. Additional security measures listed under mitigation techniques such as M1048, M1037, M1030, M1016, M1026, M1050, M1035, and M1051 may further reduce exposure to this vulnerability.
